User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the CareerCraft AI application, storing their profile information and subscription plan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for login and notifications.

plan.description: The subscription plan of the user (e.g., 'free', 'premium').

onboardingStep.description: Tracks the user's progress through the initial onboarding flow.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

ModelInfo.title: ModelInfo
ModelInfo.description: Metadata about an AI model fetched from the provider.

ModelRegistry.title: ModelRegistry
ModelRegistry.description: A collection of available AI models stored in the database.

MasterResume.title: MasterResume
MasterResume.description: Represents the user's primary, comprehensive resume, often built from an uploaded document and AI restructuring. This serves as the foundational resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MasterResume entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this master resume. (Relationship: User 1:N MasterResume)

title.description: A user-defined title for the master resume (e.g., 'My Core Resume').

summary.description: A concise overview or professional summary of the user's experience and goals.

originalFileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded document (e.g., 'John_Doe_CV.pdf') from which this master resume was generated. Null if created from scratch.

originalFileUrl.description: URL to the stored original resume file in cloud storage. Null if created from scratch.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the master resume was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the master resume was last updated.

WorkExperience.title: WorkExperience
WorkExperience.description: Represents a single work experience entry within a master resume. These entries are the building blocks for targeted resumes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkExperience entity.

masterResumeId.description: Reference to the MasterResume this experience belongs to. (Relationship: MasterResume 1:N WorkExperience)

jobTitle.description: The title of the position held.

companyName.description: The name of the company or organization.

location.description: The location of the job (city, state, country).

endDate.description: The end date of employment. Can be null if currently employed.

description.description: A detailed description of responsibilities and achievements in the role, typically as bullet points or paragraphs.

order.description: The display order of this experience within the resume section, lower numbers appear first.

Education.title: Education
Education.description: Represents an educational background entry within a master resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Education entity.

masterResumeId.description: Reference to the MasterResume this education entry belongs to. (Relationship: MasterResume 1:N Education)

degree.description: The degree or qualification obtained (e.g., 'B.Sc. Computer Science', 'MBA').

institutionName.description: The name of the educational institution.

graduationDate.description: The date of graduation or completion.

description.description: Additional details like honors, relevant coursework, or GPA.

order.description: The display order of this education entry within the resume section, lower numbers appear first.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a skill listed within a master resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

masterResumeId.description: Reference to the MasterResume this skill belongs to. (Relationship: MasterResume 1:N Skill)

name.description: The name of the skill (e.g., 'Python', 'Project Management').

proficiency.description: The user's proficiency level in the skill (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Expert').

category.description: A categorization of the skill (e.g., 'Programming Languages', 'Soft Skills', 'Tools').

order.description: The display order of this skill within its category or the skills section, lower numbers appear first.

JobSpec.title: JobSpec
JobSpec.description: Represents a job specification uploaded or linked by the user for resume targeting.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobSpec entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded this job specification. (Relationship: User 1:N JobSpec)

title.description: A user-friendly title for the job specification (e.g., 'Software Engineer - Google').

sourceUrl.description: The web URL if the job spec was provided as a link. Null if uploaded as a file.

uploadedFileName.description: The original file name if the job spec was uploaded as a document. Null if provided as a URL.

uploadedFileUrl.description: URL to the stored uploaded job specification file in cloud storage. Null if provided as a URL.

extractedText.description: The full text content extracted from the job specification, used for AI analysis and matching.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the job specification was created/uploaded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the job specification was last updated.

TargetedResume.title: TargetedResume
TargetedResume.description: Represents a version of a resume tailored specifically for a particular job specification, derived from a MasterResume. It has its own summary and can have modified sections.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TargetedResume entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this targeted resume. (Relationship: User 1:N TargetedResume)

masterResumeId.description: Reference to the MasterResume from which this targeted resume was derived. (Relationship: MasterResume 1:N TargetedResume)

jobSpecId.description: Reference to the JobSpec this resume is targeting. (Relationship: JobSpec 1:N TargetedResume)

title.description: A descriptive title for this targeted resume (e.g., 'Software Engineer Resume for Google').

customSummary.description: A summary specifically tailored for this targeted job application, potentially AI-generated.

matchScore.description: An AI-generated score (e.g., 0-100) indicating how well this resume matches the job specification.

aiAnalysisReport.description: A detailed report from the AI explaining the match assessment and suggestions for this targeted resume.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the targeted resume was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the targeted resume was last updated.

TargetedWorkExperience.title: TargetedWorkExperience
TargetedWorkExperience.description: A work experience entry specifically tailored for a TargetedResume, derived from a MasterResume's WorkExperience.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TargetedWorkExperience entity.

targetedResumeId.description: Reference to the TargetedResume this experience belongs to. (Relationship: TargetedResume 1:N TargetedWorkExperience)

masterWorkExperienceId.description: Reference to the original WorkExperience in the MasterResume from which this entry was derived. (Relationship: WorkExperience 1:N TargetedWorkExperience)

jobTitle.description: The tailored title of the position held.

location.description: The tailored location of the job.

startDate.description: The start date of employment for this entry.

endDate.description: The end date of employment for this entry. Can be null if currently employed.

description.description: A detailed description of responsibilities and achievements in the role, tailored to the job specification.

order.description: The display order of this tailored experience within the targeted resume section.

TargetedEducation.title: TargetedEducation
TargetedEducation.description: An education entry specifically tailored for a TargetedResume, derived from a MasterResume's Education.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TargetedEducation entity.

targetedResumeId.description: Reference to the TargetedResume this education entry belongs to. (Relationship: TargetedResume 1:N TargetedEducation)

masterEducationId.description: Reference to the original Education in the MasterResume from which this entry was derived. (Relationship: Education 1:N TargetedEducation)

location.description: The tailored location of the institution.

graduationDate.description: The graduation or completion date for this entry.

description.description: Additional details like honors, relevant coursework, or GPA, tailored to the job specification.

order.description: The display order of this tailored education entry within the targeted resume section.

TargetedSkill.title: TargetedSkill
TargetedSkill.description: A skill entry specifically tailored for a TargetedResume, derived from a MasterResume's Skill.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TargetedSkill entity.

targetedResumeId.description: Reference to the TargetedResume this skill belongs to. (Relationship: TargetedResume 1:N TargetedSkill)

masterSkillId.description: Reference to the original Skill in the MasterResume from which this entry was derived. (Relationship: Skill 1:N TargetedSkill)

name.description: The tailored name of the skill.

proficiency.description: The tailored proficiency level in the skill.

category.description: The tailored categorization of the skill.

order.description: The display order of this tailored skill within the targeted resume section.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records payment transactions made by users for premium features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the payment. (Relationship: User 1:N Payment)

plan.description: The subscription plan associated with this payment (e.g., 'premium').

paymentProviderTransactionId.description: The transaction ID from the payment provider (e.g., Stripe).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payment record was created.

AIRequest.title: AIRequest
AIRequest.description: Logs all AI-powered requests and their outcomes, such as resume suggestions, restructuring, or job spec analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIRequest entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated the AI request. (Relationship: User 1:N AIRequest)

masterResumeId.description: Reference to the MasterResume involved in the AI request, if applicable. (Relationship: MasterResume 1:N AIRequest)

jobSpecId.description: Reference to the JobSpec involved in the AI request, if applicable. (Relationship: JobSpec 1:N AIRequest)

targetedResumeId.description: Reference to the TargetedResume involved in the AI request, if applicable. (Relationship: TargetedResume 1:N AIRequest)

requestPayload.description: The input data sent to the AI model for the request, typically a JSON string of relevant data.

responseResult.description: The output or result received from the AI model, typically a JSON string of the AI's response.

status.description: The status of the AI request (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'failed').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the AI request was made.

OnlineResumePage.title: OnlineResumePage
OnlineResumePage.description: Represents a publicly shareable online version of a user's resume, which can be based on either a master or a targeted resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OnlineResumePage entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this online resume page. (Relationship: User 1:N OnlineResumePage)

masterResumeId.description: Reference to the MasterResume this online page is based on. Required if not based on a TargetedResume. (Relationship: MasterResume 1:N OnlineResumePage)

targetedResumeId.description: Reference to a specific TargetedResume this online page is based on. Required if not based on a MasterResume. (Relationship: TargetedResume 1:N OnlineResumePage)

publicUrlPath.description: A unique, user-friendly path for the public URL (e.g., 'john-doe-software-engineer'). This forms part of the shareable URL.

isPublic.description: Indicates whether the online resume page is currently publicly accessible.

themeSettings.description: JSON string storing UI customization settings for the online page (e.g., colors, fonts, layout choices).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the online resume page was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the online resume page was last updated.

LinkTreeBioPage.title: LinkTreeBioPage
LinkTreeBioPage.description: Represents a customizable 'link in bio' style page for a user to share multiple online profiles and links.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LinkTreeBioPage entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this LinkTree bio page. (Relationship: User 1:N LinkTreeBioPage)

publicUrlPath.description: A unique, user-friendly path for the public URL (e.g., 'john-doe-links'). This forms part of the shareable URL.

isPublic.description: Indicates whether the LinkTree bio page is currently publicly accessible.

title.description: A title for the LinkTree bio page (e.g., 'My Professional Links').

description.description: A short introductory description for the page.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile image displayed on the page.

themeSettings.description: JSON string storing UI customization settings for the LinkTree page (e.g., colors, fonts, button styles).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the LinkTree bio page was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the LinkTree bio page was last updated.

LinkTreeLink.title: LinkTreeLink
LinkTreeLink.description: Represents an individual link on a user's LinkTree bio page.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LinkTreeLink entity.

linkTreeBioPageId.description: Reference to the LinkTreeBioPage this link belongs to. (Relationship: LinkTreeBioPage 1:N LinkTreeLink)

label.description: The display text for the link (e.g., 'My Portfolio', 'LinkedIn Profile').

url.description: The actual URL the link points to.

order.description: The display order of this link on the page, lower numbers appear first.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, subscription plan, and onboarding status. Auth: Only the user with matching userId can read/write their own document.
Global storage for the latest fetched list of AI models. Restricted to Developer role for writing.
Stores the user's foundational, comprehensive resume. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the path can read/write. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence.

Stores individual work experience entries for a master resume. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the parent path can read/write. Inherits 'userId' and 'masterResumeId' from the path for authorization independence.

Stores individual education entries for a master resume. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the parent path can read/write. Inherits 'userId' and 'masterResumeId' from the path for authorization independence.

Stores individual skill entries for a master resume. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the parent path can read/write. Inherits 'userId' and 'masterResumeId' from the path for authorization independence.

Stores job specifications uploaded by the user for resume targeting. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the path can read/write. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence.

Stores resumes tailored for specific job specifications. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the path can read/write. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence.

Stores tailored work experience entries for a targeted resume. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the parent path can read/write. Inherits 'userId' and 'targetedResumeId' from the path for authorization independence.

Stores tailored education entries for a targeted resume. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the parent path can read/write. Inherits 'userId' and 'targetedResumeId' from the path for authorization independence.

Stores tailored skill entries for a targeted resume. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the parent path can read/write. Inherits 'userId' and 'targetedResumeId' from the path for authorization independence.
Records payment transactions made by users. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the path can read/write. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence.
Logs all AI-powered requests and their outcomes. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the path can read/write. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence.

Private collection for users to manage their online resume pages. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the path can read/write. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence. This data is mirrored to the public collection.

Publicly accessible collection for online resume pages. Document ID is 'publicUrlPath'. Auth: Read-only for all users (authenticated or unauthenticated) if 'isPublic' is true. Write access only for the 'userId' stored within the document, ensuring authorization independence for public reads and owner writes. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'isPublic' for authorization independence.

Private collection for users to manage their LinkTree bio pages. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the path can read/write. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the path for authorization independence. This data is mirrored to the public collection.

Stores individual links for a user's LinkTree bio page. Auth: Only the user specified by 'userId' in the parent path can read/write. Inherits 'userId' and 'linkTreeBioPageId' from the path for authorization independence.

Publicly accessible collection for LinkTree bio pages. Document ID is 'publicUrlPath'. Auth: Read-only for all users (authenticated or unauthenticated) if 'isPublic' is true. Write access only for the 'userId' stored within the document, ensuring authorization independence for public reads and owner writes. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'isPublic' for authorization independence.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Hierarchical Path-Based ownership, aligning with the core design principles and strategy mandates. All user-specific and private data is nested under `/users/{userId}/` to ensure that security rules can easily and efficiently verify ownership using `request.auth.uid == userId` without relying on `get()` operations. This makes rule evaluation atomic, debuggable, and performant.

To address **Authorization Independence**, for subcollections like `WorkExperience` under `MasterResume`, `userId` is implicitly available in the path (`/users/{userId}/masterResumes/{masterResumeId}/workExperiences/{workExperienceId}`). The `masterResumeId` also serves as a direct reference, making ownership clear within the nested structure. This pattern is consistently applied across all nested user-owned data.

For **public-facing data** such as `OnlineResumePage` and `LinkTreeBioPage`, a critical segregation strategy is employed. Each public entity will have two representations:
1.  A **private representation** under `/users/{userId}/onlineResumePages/{onlineResumePageId}` (and similar for `LinkTreeBioPage`), where the user can manage and edit their content. These documents explicitly contain `userId` as an attribute.
2.  A **public, read-only representation** under top-level collections like `/onlineResumePagesPublic/{publicUrlPath}` and `/linkTreeBioPagesPublic/{publicUrlPath}`. These public documents will use the `publicUrlPath` as their document ID for direct URL access. CRITICALLY, these public documents will denormalize essential authorization fields (`userId` and `isPublic`) directly into their content. This allows public read rules to simply check `request.auth != null || resource.data.isPublic == true` and owner write rules to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the private document. Any updates to the private representation will trigger a Cloud Function to update its corresponding public mirror, ensuring data consistency while maintaining authorization independence in rules.

**Querying and Authorization Posture (QAPs)** are directly supported by this design:
*   **Private Data:** `list` operations on collections like `/users/{userId}/masterResumes` or `/users/{userId}/jobSpecs` are inherently secure. Rules will enforce that `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`, ensuring users only retrieve their own data. This eliminates the need for rules to act as filters.
*   **Public Data:** `list` operations on `/onlineResumePagesPublic` or `/linkTreeBioPagesPublic` can be performed by anyone, and rules can allow reads for documents where `isPublic` is true. `request.auth.uid` would only be required for modifications by the owner. By using `publicUrlPath` as the document ID, direct lookups for a specific public page are efficient and secure for unauthenticated users.

DBAC is handled by storing the user's `plan` directly in the `/users/{userId}` document. Rules can directly reference `get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.plan` for feature gating, a standard and acceptable pattern for global user attributes.
